questions.question: Which two temples did the author visit in Kathmandu?
questions.explanation: The author visits a famous Hindu temple and a Buddhist shrine during his stay in Kathmandu.

questions.question: Why were some visitors not allowed to enter the Pashupatinath temple?
questions.correct_answer: Because only Hindus were allowed to enter
questions.explanation: The temple has a rule restricting entry only to Hindu devotees.

questions.question: What was the atmosphere like at Pashupatinath temple?
questions.explanation: Many priests, devotees, animals, and tourists created confusion and noise.

questions.question: How was the atmosphere at Baudhnath different from Pashupatinath?
questions.explanation: The Buddhist shrine offered a quiet and serene environment compared to the crowded temple.

questions.question: What items were sold in the markets around Baudhnath?
questions.correct_answer: Felt bags, Tibetan prints, and silver jewellery
questions.explanation: These items were sold by Tibetan immigrants in small shops around the shrine.

questions.question: What attracted the author’s attention in the square?
questions.explanation: The sound of the flute music stood out among the noise of the busy streets.

questions.question: How did the flute seller sell his flutes?
questions.correct_answer: By playing them calmly instead of shouting
questions.explanation: He quietly played the flute and occasionally sold one without aggressively advertising.

questions.question: What does the author say about the flute as a musical instrument?
questions.correct_answer: It exists in many cultures around the world
questions.explanation: The author lists several types of flutes from different countries.

questions.question: Why did the author decide to return home by flight?
questions.correct_answer: Because he was tired and homesick
questions.explanation: Although he considered a longer travel route, he finally chose the easier option.

questions.question: What is the main theme of the lesson 'Kathmandu'?
questions.correct_answer: The cultural diversity and lively atmosphere of Kathmandu
questions.explanation: The lesson shows the contrast between busy religious places and peaceful spiritual sites.

notes.title: Religious Diversity
notes.content: Kathmandu is a place where different religions such as Hinduism and Buddhism coexist peacefully.

notes.title: Universal Power of Music
notes.content: The author explains that music, especially the flute, connects people across cultures.

facts.fact: Pashupatinath is one of the most sacred Hindu temples in Nepal.
The author hears many types of flutes used across cultures.
